text:
Once upon a time in a quiet little town named Everdell, there was a young girl named Lily.

Lily had a brilliant mind for storytelling. Every evening after school, she would spin tales so captivating that children from near and far would gather around her fireplace. Her little brother, Toby, would sit by her side, his tiny feet tucked into her scarf, as they listened to stories of dragons flying backwards or witches on broomsticks dancing. Those stories were like friendships she didn't have.

One twilight sky, Lily crafted a tale about a magical forest filled with talking animals. She painted the creatures living harmoniously, each helping one another. The wise oak spoke knowledge lost since ancient times; the playful squirrel delivered amusing secrets from deep within the forest.

One night, after her parents had retired to sleep, Lily sat by the fireplace. Her heart swelled with the magic she weaved. She imagined them, her forest friends, watching her, approving perhaps. And then she dreamed another
